# Dr. Li Wei — Dental Care, Hong Kong (Full Profile)

> Dr. Li Wei (李蔚) is a general, cosmetic and implant dentist in Hong Kong with over 12 years of clinical experience. She provides gentle, honest dental care for the whole family, at a calm pace, with every option and cost explained before treatment begins. Consultations are available in Cantonese, English, and Mandarin.

## About Dr. Li Wei
Dr. Li Wei became a dentist because she believes good dental care should never feel rushed or frightening. Over more than a decade in practice, she has cared for families across Hong Kong — from a child's first visit to an adult's full-mouth restoration. She keeps her patient list personal, so you see the same dentist each time and the relationship builds over the years. Her training spans general, cosmetic and implant dentistry, and she continues to study each year to keep her techniques current and comfortable. Most of her patients arrive by word of mouth.

## Care philosophy
- Gentle, at your pace: anxious patients and children are welcome; nothing proceeds until you are comfortable and understand what will happen.
- Prevent before you treat: regular check-ups and honest advice keep small problems from becoming big ones.
- Clear on options and cost: you always know your choices, what each involves, and what it costs, before you decide. No surprises, no pressure.

## Treatments

### 01 General & Preventive Dentistry
Routine examinations, professional scaling and polishing, tooth-coloured fillings, and root canal treatment. Focus on catching issues early and keeping natural teeth healthy for as long as possible. A check-up and cleaning every six months is recommended for most patients.

### 02 Cosmetic Dentistry
Professional teeth whitening, porcelain veneers, and cosmetic bonding. A natural look is favoured — enhancing your own teeth rather than replacing their character. Veneers are thin custom shells bonded to the front of teeth to improve shape, colour, or alignment, correcting chips, gaps, and staining with minimal change to the tooth beneath.

### 03 Dental Implants & Restorations
Replacing missing or damaged teeth with dental implants, crowns, bridges, and dentures, restoring both function and appearance. A dental implant is a small titanium post placed in the jaw to act as a replacement tooth root, topped with a crown; it looks and functions like a natural tooth and does not rely on neighbouring teeth for support.

### 04 Clear Aligners & Orthodontics
Straightening teeth discreetly with clear aligners (e.g. Invisalign), suitable for many cases of crowding, spacing, and mild-to-moderate bite issues. Clear aligners are a series of removable, near-invisible trays that gently move teeth into position over time; they can be taken out to eat and clean. Suitability is confirmed after an examination and scan. Complex cases may need fixed braces or a specialist referral, which Dr. Li Wei will discuss honestly.

## Credentials & trust
- Registered dental surgeon (BDS), holding to professional standards of care and hygiene.
- Over 12 years in clinical practice, treating patients of every age.
- Continuing education in implants, cosmetic techniques, and clear aligner therapy.
- Referral-led practice — most new patients arrive through the recommendation of an existing one.

## Frequently asked questions

**What does Dr. Li Wei treat?**
General and preventive dentistry, cosmetic dentistry, dental implants and restorations, and clear aligner orthodontics — everything from a routine check-up, cleaning, and fillings to whitening, veneers, implants, crowns, and teeth straightening for teens and adults, all under one trusted dentist.

**How often should I have a check-up?**
For most people, a check-up and professional cleaning every six months keeps teeth and gums healthy and catches problems early. Those with gum disease, high decay risk, or ongoing treatment may be seen more often.

**Are clear aligners suitable for me?**
They suit many cases of crowding, spacing, and mild-to-moderate bite issues; suitability is confirmed after an examination and scan. Some complex cases are better treated with fixed braces or need a specialist, and Dr. Li Wei will say so honestly.

**Do you see nervous patients and children?**
Yes, and they are very welcome. Dr. Li Wei works at a gentle pace and never rushes. For anxious adults and young children, the first visit is often just a gentle look and a chat.

**Will I know the cost before treatment?**
Always. After examining you, Dr. Li Wei explains your options and gives a clear estimate before you decide anything. Exact fees depend on your diagnosis, so they are confirmed in person rather than quoted online.

**How do I book an appointment?**
Via the booking form on the website or a WhatsApp message. Every enquiry is answered personally, in English or Chinese.

## Contact
- Booking: website form or WhatsApp.
- Based in Hong Kong.
- Languages: Cantonese, English, Mandarin.

## Important notes
This information is for general reference only and does not constitute dental or medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment, nor a guarantee of any result. Suitability for any treatment, and its cost, can only be determined at an in-person examination. Any procedures carry risks that will be discussed during a consultation. Please seek personalised advice from Dr. Li Wei before making any decision about your dental care.
